activities_projects.title: Comparative Scientist Study
steps: Select two scientists from different eras
Compare their discoveries and impact
Present a short report or chart
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands evolution of science through different contributors
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison and clarity of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Experiment Design Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Learn to design a simple experiment
steps: Choose a small scientific question (e.g., effect of sunlight on plants)
Plan an experiment with steps and controls
Record expected outcomes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops planning and critical thinking skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Feasibility and clarity of experiment design (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Timeline of Discoveries with Causes and Effects
activities_projects.objective: Link scientific discoveries with their societal impact
steps: List major discoveries (e.g., steam engine, electricity, antibiotics)
Write brief note on cause and effect of each discovery
Draw timeline connecting them chronologically
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Shows cause-effect relationship of scientific progress

activities_projects.title: Mini Research on Modern Inventions
steps: Select a modern invention (e.g., smartphone, renewable energy device)
Write about inventor, principle, and uses
Prepare a short presentation for class

activities_projects.title: Observation and Recording of Scientific Phenomena
steps: Observe a natural or physical phenomenon (e.g., plant growth, shadows, magnetism)
Record daily observations for a week
Analyze changes and possible causes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances systematic observation and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Consistency and depth of observations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role of Tools in Scientific Experiments
materials: Pictures or small models of tools
steps: Select 3 scientific tools from different eras
Describe their use and how they improved experiments
Present findings in a chart or report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Comprehends evolution and significance of scientific tools
activities_projects.evaluation: Detail, clarity, and relevance of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate: Science and Society
activities_projects.objective: Discuss pros and cons of scientific progress
steps: Form two groups – one supporting benefits, one discussing potential harms
Research examples and prepare arguments
Conduct a debate in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Encourages critical thinking and understanding societal impact of science
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Model of a Discovery Process
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the steps of scientific discovery
steps: Select a discovery (e.g., microscope, penicillin)
Create a model showing how it was developed
Explain the process to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Shows understanding of the experimental and iterative nature of science
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and accuracy of model (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Science Newspaper Column
steps: Select a recent scientific development
Write a short newspaper-style article
Include key facts and possible future implications
activities_projects.evaluation: Content accuracy, clarity, and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Group Poster: Evolution of Science
steps: Divide class into groups
Each group selects a period in science history
Create a poster showing major discoveries and scientists
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances teamwork, creativity, and understanding of historical evolution
